metadata.title: Solution Architecture Document — MyMedwick Patient Portal

documentControl.purpose: Describe the architecture of the MyMedwick patient portal serving Medwick Healthcare Trust patients and clinical staff, with emphasis on patient safety, MediCore DSPT compliance, and integration with MediCore Spine, e-Referral Service, and the Trust EPR.
documentControl.scope: Patient-facing portal (web and mobile), associated API services, integration adapters to MediCore national services and the Trust EPR, and clinical safety controls. Excludes the EPR itself and primary-care GP systems.

executiveSummary.solutionOverview: MyMedwick is the patient-facing portal for Medwick Healthcare Trust, a MediCore acute Trust serving ~580,000 patients across three hospital sites in the East of England. It provides appointment management, letters and results visibility, secure messaging with care teams, repeat prescription requests, and digital pre-assessment forms. It runs on Azure UK South (paired with UK West for DR), built on Azure App Service, Azure SQL, Azure Service Bus, and Azure API Management. MediCore Spine integration uses FHIR R4 via MESH.

businessContext.description: DNA rate of 9.4% costs the Trust ~£4.8m/year; portal-based reminders target 2pp reduction
Trust digital strategy commits to MediCore Login and digital-first patient access
Patient Knows Best contract ends 2026-Q3; in-house build reduces run cost from £680k to £420k/year
CS-129/0160-compliant clinical safety case required for digital appointment communication

inScope: Patient registration and identity verification via MediCore Login
Appointment booking, rescheduling, cancellation, and reminders
Secure messaging with named clinical teams
MediCore Spine, e-RS, and GP Connect integration
executiveSummary.currentState: Patient Knows Best (PKB) provides current portal capability via SaaS; ~40,000 patients enrolled. Limited MediCore Login; no FHIR connectivity to Trust EPR. Trust pays £680k/year and faces contract end Q3 2026.

keyDecisions.decision: Build vs buy: in-house build on Azure
keyDecisions.rationale: PKB contract end + budget reduction; Azure landing zone; reuse of MediCore Login and Spine integrations
keyDecisions.implication: Higher capex; longer time to live; Trust accountability for clinical safety

keyDecisions.rationale: Trust standard; Azure AD B2C tenant; UK Sovereign Cloud lineage
keyDecisions.implication: All compute on Azure UK South + UK West

keyDecisions.rationale: Lower ops burden for small platform team

keyDecisions.implication: Adapter complexity contained in Spine integration service

keyDecisions.rationale: Patient data must remain in UK per MediCore DSPT
keyDecisions.implication: All primary and DR regions in UK

compliance.regulatedActivityDetails: MyMedwick supports CQC-regulated activities including treatment and diagnostic services. MediCore DSPT compliance is mandatory.

regulatoryRequirements.name: MediCore DSPT
regulatoryRequirements.impact: Annual self-assessment; underpins MediCore Spine connectivity

regulatoryRequirements.name: UK GDPR / Data Protection Act 2018
regulatoryRequirements.impact: DPIA; lawful basis Article 6(1)(e)/9(2)(h); restricted right-to-erasure due to clinical record retention

regulatoryRequirements.name: CS-129 / CS-160
regulatoryRequirements.applicability: Mandatory — health IT system that may affect patient safety
regulatoryRequirements.impact: Clinical safety case, hazard log, post-deployment surveillance

regulatoryRequirements.name: MediCore Login Identity Assurance Level P9
regulatoryRequirements.applicability: Mandatory — patient access to clinical data
regulatoryRequirements.impact: All patient access requires MediCore Login P9 verification

regulatoryRequirements.name: CQC Well-Led / Responsive
regulatoryRequirements.impact: Accessibility, equity of access, governance evidence

designPatterns.rationale: Reduces ops complexity for small platform team; bounded modules support future split
APIM provides rate limiting, mTLS, observability, Spine integration policy
Service Bus for appointment/results events drives notifications without coupling
Spine outages must degrade gracefully; portal must remain available

externalIntegrations.purpose: Patient identity verification at MediCore Login Identity Assurance Level P9
Patient demographic lookup, e-Referral, GP Connect record summary
SMS appointment reminders and email notifications
FHIR R4 read/write of appointments, observations, document references

dataView.dataSovereigntyDetails: All patient data held in Azure UK regions only. MediCore Spine messages processed in UK only.

constraints.constraint: All patient data must remain in UK
MediCore Login Identity Assurance Level P9 mandatory for patient access
constraints.impactOnDesign: Identity flow must complete P9 verification before any clinical data is accessible

constraints.constraint: CS-129 clinical safety case required pre go-live
constraints.impactOnDesign: Hazard log, mitigation, and CSO sign-off gate go-live
Migration must complete with 3-month parallel run before contract end

assumptions.assumption: MediCore Spine FHIR APIs remain stable for the contract lifetime
Trust EPR (EpicCare) supports FHIR R4 export at sufficient throughput

risks.mitigationPlan: End-to-end encryption; field-level encryption for messaging; quarterly DSPT review; ICO breach response runbook (72h notification)

risks.riskEvent: Clinical harm from incorrect appointment / results display
risks.mitigationPlan: CS-129/0160 clinical safety case; CSO oversight; hazard log MHT-HAZ-LOG-0208; idempotent ID matching; weekly Datix review
Cached recent demographics; degraded mode hides Spine-dependent features; status banner runbook within 5 minutes

risks.riskEvent: PKB-to-MyMedwick migration loses patient data or consent
risks.mitigationPlan: 3-month parallel run; cohort migration with reconciliation; explicit re-consent for messaging carry-over; rollback to PKB available
Quarterly internal DSPT review; pre-audit gap analysis annually; evidence library maintained continuously

glossary.definition: Clinical Risk Management — manufacturer responsibilities for health IT systems
Clinical Risk Management — deployment and use of health IT systems
Data Security and Protection Toolkit — annual MediCore information governance assessment
Electronic Patient Record — Trust's clinical record system (EpicCare)
Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources — HL7 standard
Message Exchange for Social and Health — MediCore Digital messaging service
Personal Demographics Service — national MediCore patient demographic register
MediCore Spine — central national MediCore infrastructure for clinical messaging and identity
